data.title: Delcath Systems Q4 2024: Unraveling Contradictions in R&D Spending, Treatment Schedules, and European Revenue Growth
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Delcath Systems, Inc.'s latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: R&D spending expectations, impact of holidays on treatment schedules, number of treatments per patient, center activation strategies, revenue growth expectations in Europe, and reimbursement process challenges:



U.S. Launch Success of HEPZATO:
- Delcath Systems generated $32.3 million in revenue from HEPZATO in the U.S. for the year 2024, including $13.7 million in the fourth quarter.
- The success was driven by the U.S. launch of HEPZATO in January 2024, which provided a novel treatment to patients, and the securing of permanent J-codes and NTAP payments.

European Market Expansion:
- European CHEMOSAT volumes grew by 137% in 2024, with Germany up 75% and other markets doubling year-over-year.
- This expansion was supported by strategic clinical trials and publications, which are important for supporting further reimbursement efforts.

Financial Stability and R&D Investment:
- The company ended 2024 with $53.2 million in cash and investments, achieving positive adjusted EBITDA of $4.6 million in the fourth quarter.
- Financial stability was driven by receipts from warrant exercises and a focus on R&D to maximize the potential of the hepatic delivery system.

Centers of Expertise and Market Penetration:
- Delcath activated three new U.S. treatment centers in the fourth quarter and plans for more centers to increase to 30 by year-end.
- This expansion is supported by a bolstered commercial team and efforts to integrate HEPZATO into treatment algorithms, enhancing clinical traction and referrals.

data.title: NACCO Industries' Q4 2024: Diverging Views on Coal Strategy, Growth Prospects, and Mining Volumes
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in NACCO Industries, Inc.'s latest 2024 Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Coal Business Strategy and Financial Performance, Future Growth Prospects, Mississippi Lignite Mining Company (MLMC) Volumes, and North American Mining's Business Strategy:



Strong Financial Performance in Coal Mining Segment:
- NACCO Industries reported a robust 2024 fourth quarter net income of $7.6 million and full-year net income of $33.7 million.
- The Coal Mining segment's segment-adjusted EBITDA more than quadrupled from 2023.
- The improvement was primarily due to increased earnings at Mississippi Lignite Mining Company, driven by business interruption insurance income and reduced customer demand.

North American Mining Segment Growth:
- North American Mining reported operating profit of $800,000 in 2024 Q4, compared to a $600,000 operating loss in 2023.
- Full year operating profit was up 72% compared with 2023, with net present value of after-tax cash flows reaching approximately $20 million over the contract terms.
- Growth was attributed to new contracts and strategic projects that improved profitability.

Minerals Management Segment Expansion:
- Minerals Management invested an additional $15.7 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 in oil and natural gas assets.
- This investment is expected to be accretive to future earnings, aligning with the company's long-term strategy to expand mineral interests and increase profitability.

Cash Flow and Liquidity Position:
- NACCO Industries ended the year with consolidated cash of approximately $73 million and debt of $99.5 million.
- The company expects significant annual cash flow generation in 2025, due to operational and strategic improvements across its segments.

data.title: Coldware Token Surges 1300% as it Enters Mobile Phone Market
data.description:
Coldware (COLD) Token has recently experienced a remarkable surge of 1,300%, marking a significant milestone as it prepares to enter the mobile phone market. This move represents a strategic expansion for coldware, transitioning from its roots in the DeFi and blockchain space into the consumer electronics industry. This bold initiative positions Coldware to challenge established cryptocurrencies such as Litecoin (LTC) and Near Protocol (NEAR).

Coldware is leveraging its blockchain technology to develop mobile devices that integrate iot (Internet of Things) and DePIN (Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Networks) solutions. This integration allows Coldware to offer smartphones that not only function as mobile devices but also as part of a decentralized ecosystem. This move has garnered attention within both the cryptocurrency and tech industries, highlighting Coldware's potential to disrupt both the DeFi and consumer electronics sectors in the coming years.

As Coldware (COLD) surges by 1,300%, Litecoin (LTC) and Near Protocol (NEAR) are facing new competition in both the blockchain and mobile markets. Litecoin, known for its peer-to-peer payment capabilities, is increasingly overshadowed by newer technologies like Coldware. Despite Litecoin’s consistent performance and growing recognition in DeFi, Coldware’s focus on real-world applications, including IoT and mobile devices, makes it a formidable competitor.

Similarly, Near Protocol continues to perform well within the DeFi space, but Coldware (COLD)’s ability to provide a mobile-first blockchain solution gives it a distinct edge. This positions Coldware as a leader in the DeFi and IoT sectors, with its focus on real-world utility and mobile expansion. As Litecoin and Near Protocol face competition from emerging players, Coldware’s innovative approach and upcoming mobile phone launch are expected to continue disrupting markets in 2025 and beyond.

data.title: DADDY Solana Meme Coin Surges Amid Tate Legal Drama
data.description:
The recent surge in interest for the DADDY Solana meme coin is directly linked to the escalating legal troubles surrounding controversial influencer Andrew Tate. As the Tates face fresh criminal inquiries in the US, the market display for DADDY reflects the speculative nature often seen in meme coin investments. Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier stated, “These guys have themselves publicly admitted to participating in what very much appears to be soliciting, trafficking, preying upon women,” highlighting the serious nature of the ongoing investigation.

This article explores the volatile movement of the DADDY token amid Andrew Tate’s escalating legal issues, reflecting the meme coin market’s speculative behaviors. The recent legal issues surrounding Andrew Tate, known for his polarizing views and influence, have sparked significant activity in the cryptocurrency market, particularly with the DADDY token. Following news of Tate’s new criminal investigation in Florida, DADDY experienced a brief surge of 11.46%, peaking at a market cap of $34 million. This price movement is noteworthy, especially in the context of an otherwise stagnant meme coin market.

The DADDY token has gained attention as a prime example of how public controversies can drive speculative trading in cryptocurrencies. With the Tates facing serious allegations of human trafficking and other charges, it creates an engaging narrative that attracts traders. Such spikes in price often correlate with heightened media coverage, illustrating a classic reaction in crypto markets—where emotional sentiment can lead to rapid price fluctuations.

Historically, the DADDY token has shown an ability to react sharply to news related to its founder. For instance, shortly after Tate’s previous arrest in Romania, the token experienced significant volatility, with a remarkable 78% surge when Tate first traveled to Florida. However, the current performance indicates that while hype might provide temporary gains, sustainability remains a challenge. As of now, DADDY’s value is down 85% from its all-time high of $209 million market cap, reached in mid-2023.

Despite reactive price spikes, the future of DADDY investors remains uncertain. The ongoing criminal investigations and charges against Andrew Tate mean that his name will likely remain in the spotlight, potentially giving the DADDY token more moments of speculative interest. Nevertheless, without substantive developments or community support, long-term investors could face significant risks. Stakeholders should approach this token with

data.title: Fortuna Mining Corp.'s Q4 2024 Earnings Call: Key Contradictions on San Jose Mine, Power Stability, and Milling Rates
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Fortuna Mining Corp.'s latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: San Jose Mine's future, electricity supply stability in Côte d’Ivoire, San Jose Mine operational status, and Yaramoko's milling rate expectations:



Financial Performance and Cash Flow:
- Fortuna Mining reported record free cash flow from operations of $19.6 million in Q4, representing a 69% increase versus Q3.
- The growth was driven by stable costs and rising gold prices, leading to a higher operating cash flow margin from 33% to 50%.

Gold Production and Costs:
- Gold production from Séguéla and Yaramoko mines exceeded annual production guidance, with Séguéla delivering 137,781 ounces and Yaramoko 116,206 ounces in 2024.
- Séguéla's cash cost for the year outperformed guidance at $584 per ounce, while Yaramoko achieved a cash cost of $860 per ounce for the year.

Latin American Operations:
- Lindero Mine in Argentina produced 26,806 ounces of gold in Q4, a 10% improvement over the previous quarter.
- The increase was due to higher rate of pregnant solution percolation, and the beginning of ore placement on the new leach pad expansion area.

Exploration and Project Development:
- Fortuna Mining invested $49 million in mineral exploration and new project development in 2024, with plans to continue aggressive investments in 2025.
- Exploration targets include Kingfisher and Sunbird Deep at the Séguéla Mine Camp, and Diamba Sud project in Senegal, aiming for high value opportunities.

data.title: "Microsoft (MSFT) Bets Big on AI in South Africa-Here's Why It Matters"
data.description:
Microsoft's recent investment of over R5 billion in South Africa to expand its AI and cloud infrastructure is a bold move that could reshape the continent's technological landscape. But why is this investment so significant, and what does it mean for Microsoft's global strategy and the future of AI in Africa?

First, let's consider the context. Africa is a continent on the cusp of a digital revolution, with a median age of 19 and 60% of its population under 35. This youthful demographic is a goldmine of potential, and microsoft is betting big on harnessing this talent to drive AI innovation. By 2030, Africa's growing tech talent could solidify its role as a key player in the global economy, and Microsoft's investment is a strategic move to position itself at the forefront of this transformation.



Microsoft's AI skilling initiative, which aims to equip one million South Africans with critical digital skills by 2026, is a cornerstone of this strategy. This initiative is not just about training people; it's about empowering individuals and businesses to thrive in an increasingly AI-powered world. As Brad Smith, Vice Chair and President of Microsoft, noted, "These are precisely the certificates and the skills that win people's jobs." By investing in human capital, Microsoft is not only fostering innovation and economic growth but also creating a talent pool that can drive its AI and cloud services forward, both locally and globally.

But Microsoft's commitment to responsible AI and ethical considerations is what sets this investment apart. The company's AI initiatives in South Africa are governed by its AI Access Principles, which emphasize the ethical use of AI. This commitment to ethical AI practices is not just a PR stunt; it's a reflection of Microsoft's global mission to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As Edward Kieswetter, Commissioner of SARS, highlighted, "We must come to terms that our future is a co-existence with AI agents that in many ways will replace some of the work we do, augment all our work and significantly enhance our abilities as a species." This approach reflects Microsoft's global emphasis on ethical AI practices.

However, Microsoft's investment in South Africa is not without its challenges. The digital divide is a real concern, and there is a risk that AI technologies could exacerbate existing inequalities. As President Cyril Ramaphosa acknowledged, "We need to ensure that AI doesn’t deepen the digital divide, but that it actually narrows it and eventually eliminates it." This requires a concerted effort to ensure that AI technologies are inclusive and equitable, benefiting all sectors of society.

Moreover, there is a need for continuous investment in infrastructure and skills development. While Microsoft's current investment is substantial, sustained funding and support will be necessary to maintain and expand AI capabilities. As Lillian Barnard, President of Microsoft Africa, stated, "AI is our opportunity to help Africa leapfrog. Microsoft is excited about the artificial intelligence opportunity in Africa; we are truly in the next wave of innovation called artificial intelligence transformation."

In summary, Microsoft's investment in AI and cloud services in South Africa is a strategic move that aligns with the continent's broader economic and technological development goals. By focusing on AI and cloud services, Microsoft is not only enhancing its market presence and influence but also fostering innovation, efficiency, and economic growth. However, addressing the digital divide, ensuring sustained investment, and strengthening regulatory frameworks will be crucial in overcoming potential challenges and realizing the full potential of AI in South Africa. As Microsoft continues to invest in AI and cloud services, it is positioning itself as a global leader in responsible AI development, with a commitment to ethical practices and inclusivity that sets it apart from its competitors.

data.title: Graphs Worth 1000 Words: What Are the Impacts of Trump's Tariffs?
data.description:
Former President Donald Trump is once again wielding tariffs as a policy tool, but this time, it may backfire. A recent New York Fed survey revealed that businesses expect cost increases in 2025 to surpass those of the previous year. This will likely add further pressure to domestic inflation.

Additionally, research shows that Trump's tariff policies could cost the average American middle-class household an extra $1,200 annually. Lower-income groups would bear a disproportionately higher burden, while wealthier Americans would actually benefit from the extension of Trump's tax cuts. In fact, all Americans—except for the richest 20%—will pay more in tariffs than they will receive from Trump's tax reductions.

Which Industries Will Be Affected?

As shown in the following chart, if Trump imposes a 25% tariff on Canada, U.S. energy prices will spike significantly—energy being a key driver of inflation.

The North American automotive industry is another major concern, as supply chains across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico are deeply intertwined. Over 80% of critical auto components imported by U.S. manufacturers come from Canada and Mexico. Imposing tariffs on these goods would, in effect, be a tax on American businesses and consumers.

Economic Impact of a 25% Tariff on Canada & Mexico

GDP: A 0.25 percentage point decline in U.S. GDP; if Canada and Mexico retaliate, the decline could exceed 0.3 percentage points.

Employment: A 0.11% reduction in U.S. jobs; with retaliation, job losses could rise to 0.25%.

Inflation: U.S. inflation could increase by more than 1.3 percentage points; however, if Canada and Mexico retaliate, inflation might rise by a lesser 0.77 percentage points due to reduced exports.

Where Do U.S. Tariffs Stand Today?

Under Trump's aggressive tariff strategy, 42% of total U.S. imports are now subject to tariffs—the highest level since World War II.

To put things into perspective, the current U.S. average tariff rate is just 3%—one of the lowest among developed economies. A sharp escalation in tariffs would mark a dramatic shift in trade policy.

Market Reaction: Investors on Edge

The uncertainty surrounding Trump's tariff policies has left U.S. stock market investors uneasy. The S&P 500 has posted negative returns this year, significantly underperforming both Chinese and European markets. goldman sachs notes that Tuesday saw the highest volume of VIX call options purchased in a single session since last September—suggesting that traders are hedging against heightened volatility.

Interestingly, this year's U.S. market trajectory closely mirrors 2019, during the height of the U.S.-China trade war.

Another notable trend: historically, the third year of a bull market tends to yield lackluster returns. Since the bear market of 2022, the current bull cycle began in 2023—making 2025 its third year.

While historical patterns can be intriguing, fundamental analysis remains key. Goldman Sachs' valuation model suggests that U.S. stock valuations are fair, whereas emerging markets—excluding China—appear significantly undervalued.

Earnings Outlook: Small-Cap Stocks Lead the Way

Despite macroeconomic concerns, analysts expect robust earnings growth for U.S. companies in 2025:

Small-cap stocks: Projected EPS growth of 36%, the highest among all asset classes.

S&P 500: Earnings expected to rise by 10%, supporting a continued bull market.

China's Economic Performance: A Decade of Outperformance

Shifting the focus to China, Chinese assets have recently rallied, though they have lagged behind global markets over the long term. However, when measured by GDP growth, China remains the global leader over the past decade.

Between 2015 and 2025, China's inflation-adjusted GDP is set to increase from $11.2 trillion to $19.5 trillion, an expansion of $8.3 trillion—outpacing even the U.S., which is expected to grow by $6.6 trillion over the same period.

In percentage terms, China's GDP is projected to rise 74% over ten years, the highest among major economies, second only to India's 77% growth. However, given China's much larger economic base, maintaining such high growth rates is particularly impressive.

Reflecting this optimism, global investors have been increasing their exposure to Chinese markets. Goldman Sachs reports that emerging market funds have, for the first time since 2022, begun overweighting Chinese assets, surpassing India in allocations.

Global Economic Developments: Japan & Gold Markets

Japan: After decades of economic stagnation, Japan may finally be exiting its deflationary trap. The country's largest labor union is demanding a 6.09% wage increase, the highest since 1993

Gold Supply: Since 2005, the global discovery of new gold reserves has halved every five years, while demand for gold continues to surge. This supply squeeze could drive prices higher in the long term.

data.title: Everything You Need to Know About Nvidia-Backed CoreWeave's IPO
data.description:
Nvidia-backed cloud provider CoreWeave filed for an IPO this week, unveiling a prospectus that highlights its stunning revenue growth while also raising some concerns.

The AI-focused company plans to trade on the Nasdaq under the ticker CRWV, aiming to raise at least $3.5 billion at a valuation of $32 billion, with the potential to push for $40 billion. In its last funding round in November 2023, the company raised $650 million at a $23 billion valuation.

Nvidia invested in CoreWeave in 2023 and holds nearly a 6% stake while also being a customer of its computing power services. The cloud provider owned a total of 250,000 nvidia GPUs, supported by more than 360 MW of active power, by the end of 2024.

With Nvidia's backing, CoreWeave's revenue surged from $228.9 million in 2023 to $1.9 billion—an eightfold increase. However, the company remains deeply unprofitable, reporting a net loss of $863 million in 2024.

What shocked the market most was that CoreWeave's three co-founders had already sold off most of their Class A shares during two funding rounds in 2023 and 2024. Regardless of the IPO's outcome, they have collectively cashed out nearly $500 million.

Specifically, co-founder, CEO, and Chairman Michael Intrator sold approximately $160 million worth of shares, while co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer Brian Venturo and co-founder and Chief Development Officer Brannin McBee sold around $177 million and $151 million, respectively.

The three founders retained less than 3% of Class A shares but maintained approximately 80% of voting rights through Class B shares, which carry 10 times the voting power.

Notably, none of the three co-founders have a technology background. They initially formed their team to capitalize on the cryptocurrency mining boom. Before launching CoreWeave, they all worked in oil industry hedge funds.

Though Nvidia is a significant investor, Microsoft is CoreWeave's largest customer, contributing 62% of its revenue last year. However, on Thursday, Microsoft reported that it has scaled back some agreements with CoreWeave due to delivery issues and missed deadlines, according to the Financial Times.

Microsoft has several ongoing contracts with CoreWeave, providing it with computing capacity from data centers in deals worth billions of dollars. Sources say these issues have undermined Microsoft's confidence in CoreWeave. However, the tech giant has maintained some existing agreements, and the two companies remain key partners.

Public records show that Microsoft had agreed to purchase over $10 billion worth of computing power from CoreWeave by 2030 under five disclosed contracts.

In its S-1 filing, CoreWeave emphasized that any negative changes in Microsoft's demand or strategic relationship would adversely impact its business, operating results, financial condition, and future prospects.

There have also been reports that Microsoft's data center strategy has shifted after ending its exclusive compute-leasing agreement with OpenAI. Last month, a TD Cowen report suggested that Microsoft was moving away from data center leasing agreements. However, sources claim that Microsoft's decision to terminate some CoreWeave contracts is unrelated to this broader shift.

In response to market speculation, Microsoft stated that its infrastructure investment plans remain on track. However, in a recent interview, CEO Satya Nadella acknowledged concerns about overbuilding AI infrastructure.

Another key point to note is that CoreWeave carries $7.9 billion in debt. However, the founders portray this debt as a feature rather than a burden, claiming they pioneered GPU infrastructure-backed lending. Their valuable GPU collection serves as collateral for financing, allowing them to secure funding to purchase even more Nvidia GPUs.

data.title: Core Natural Resources Drops 3.87% as Surge Energy Surges 10%!
"CompoSecure Beats EBITDA Estimates But Analyst Cuts Price Forecast Amid Resolute Spin-Off And Growth Outlook"
data.description:
In the ever-evolving landscape of the stock market, composecure, Inc. (CMPO) has recently made headlines with its fourth-quarter financial results and the spin-off of its subsidiary, Resolute Holdings Management, Inc. (RHLD). While the company managed to beat adjusted EBITDA expectations, analysts have cut their price targets, raising questions about the company's future prospects. Let's dive into the details and analyze what this means for investors.

The Spin-Off: A Strategic Move

On February 28, 2025, CompoSecure completed the spin-off of Resolute Holdings, creating a separate public company. Shareholders of CompoSecure received one share of Resolute Holdings for every twelve shares of CompoSecure stock they held on the record date. This move is part of CompoSecure's strategy to focus on its core business of metal payment cards, security, and authentication solutions, while Resolute Holdings will manage alternative assets.



Financial Performance: A Mixed Bag

CompoSecure reported fourth-quarter revenue of $101 million, which missed estimates but beat adjusted EBITDA expectations with $34 million. This performance was driven by lower than expected cash SG&A (excluding approximately $10 million of one-time earnout and spin-off costs), while gross profit was in line as softer topline was offset by higher margins.

Analyst Reactions: Caution Amid Optimism

Despite the beat on adjusted EBITDA, analysts have cut their price targets for CompoSecure. Needham analyst John Todaro, for instance, lowered his price target from $16 to $15, citing lower fiscal 2025 EBITDA guidance and the focus on the Resolute spin-off. This reduction suggests a more cautious outlook on the stock's near-term performance.

Long-Term Growth Prospects

While the near-term outlook may be cautious, CompoSecure's long-term growth prospects remain promising. The company expects mid-single-digit growth for 2025 net sales and adjusted EBITDA. This growth projection is supported by the company's strong free cash flow generation and the successful spin-off of Resolute Holdings, which is expected to create value for shareholders.

Key Drivers of Growth

1. International Growth: CompoSecure has seen significant international growth, with a 11% increase in international sales in FY 2024. This trend is expected to continue, contributing to overall revenue growth.

2. Product Innovations: The company's unique product innovations, such as metal payment cards and Arculus security and authentication capabilities, are expected to drive both organic and inorganic growth. For instance, Arculus turned profitable in the fourth quarter, contributing $3.5 million to adjusted EBITDA.

3. Operational Efficiency: CompoSecure is focusing on operational efficiency through the CompoSecure Operating System (COS), which aims to deliver consistent excellence across the organization. This system is expected to drive efficiency and support growth.

4. M&A Activities: The company is committed to accretive M&A activities, which are expected to contribute to revenue and earnings growth. The spin-off of Resolute Holdings is part of this strategy, allowing CompoSecure to focus on its core business and pursue growth opportunities.

5. Strong Financial Position: CompoSecure has a strong financial position, with $77.5 million of cash and cash equivalents and $197.5 million of total debt at the end of 2024. This financial strength provides the company with the flexibility to invest in growth opportunities and pursue strategic initiatives.

Conclusion

In conclusion, while the recent price target reduction by analysts may indicate short-term caution, investors should consider the strong historical revenue growth, positive analyst ratings, and future growth prospects when evaluating the current valuation of CompoSecure stock. The spin-off of Resolute Holdings and the company's financial performance are key factors that could drive future stock price appreciation. As always, it's crucial for investors to stay informed and make decisions based on a comprehensive analysis of the company's fundamentals and market conditions.

data.title: Texas Senate Approves Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Bill SB 21
data.description:
The Texas State Senate has made a significant move towards embracing cryptocurrency by approving the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Bill SB 21. The bill, which passed with a decisive 25-5 vote on March 7th, aims to establish a strategic reserve of Bitcoin for the state, allowing Texas to invest in the digital asset. This legislation, authored by Senator Charles Schwertner, removes the previous annual limit of $500 million on Bitcoin purchases, providing more flexibility for the state to engage in cryptocurrency investments.

The next step for SB 21 is its transition to the Texas House of Representatives, where designated committees will scrutinize the bill, facilitating necessary amendments and public hearings. Should the House propose modifications to SB 21, concurrence with the Senate will be essential; otherwise, a conference committee will be convened to achieve a mutually accepted version. Once reconciled, the amended bill will return for another series of votes in both chambers before reaching the desk of the Texas Governor, who retains the authority to sign it into law. This legislative progression highlights Texas’s proactive stance in the evolving landscape of digital assets.

SB 21 is part of a broader trend in the United States where several states are exploring the integration of Bitcoin into their financial strategies. New Hampshire, for instance, has recently passed a similar bill, which allows the state treasurer to allocate up to 5% of public funds into Bitcoin and other digital assets with a market capitalization of at least $500 billion. This trend is also seen in states like Utah, Arizona, and Oklahoma, where similar legislation has gained traction. However, not all states are on board with these proposals, with some rejecting them due to concerns about Bitcoin's volatility and suitability as a public asset.

The approval of SB 21 in Texas marks a pivotal moment in the state's approach to cryptocurrency. By creating a strategic reserve, Texas aims to diversify its financial portfolio and potentially benefit from the growth of Bitcoin. The bill's passage through the Senate Committee on Business and Commerce and its upcoming vote in the Senate indicate strong support for the legislation. If approved, SB 21 will move to the House of Representatives for further consideration, bringing Texas one step closer to becoming a leader in state-level cryptocurrency integration.

data.title: "Why Sleep Number (SNBR) Stock Is Falling Today"
data.description:
Listen up, folks! Today, we're diving into the nitty-gritty of why sleep number corporation (SNBR) stock is taking a nosedive. The bedding industry is in a tailspin, and sleep number is feeling the heat. Let's break it down!

First off, the numbers don't lie. Sleep Number reported a net sales decrease of 11% to $1.68 billion in 2024. That's a whopping $377 million in the fourth quarter alone, down 12% from the previous year. Ouch! And get this—they're blaming it on a lower store count, which added one to two percentage points of pressure on their sales figures. Talk about a double whammy!



But here's the kicker: despite the revenue decline, Sleep Number managed to improve its net income. They reported a net loss of $5 million for the fourth quarter, compared to a net loss of $25 million for the same period last year. That's a $20 million improvement, folks! And their adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter was $26 million, up 43% compared to the prior year. That's a 7.0% adjusted EBITDA margin, up 270 basis points versus the prior year. So, while the top line is struggling, the bottom line is showing some signs of life.

Now, let's talk about the industry trends. Tempur Sealy International Inc. (TPX) reported a stock price of $47.37 with a 0.72% increase, while Casper Sleep Inc. (CSPR) had a stock price of $6.68 with a 0.15% increase. These figures suggest that while some competitors are managing to maintain or even increase their stock prices, Sleep Number is struggling to keep up. The industry-wide weakness is further supported by the fact that Sleep Number's gross margin of 59.6% for the full year was up 190 basis points versus the prior year, but this improvement was not enough to offset the decline in sales and the net loss.

So, what's Sleep Number doing to turn things around? They've been busy transforming their operating model for greater financial resilience. Over the past 18 months, they've delivered gross margin rate improvement and operating cost reductions that were nearly double their original targets for the year. They've also reduced operating expenses by $28 million in the fourth quarter and by $88 million for the full year 2024 (before restructuring costs). This has contributed to a net loss reduction from $25 million to $5 million in the fourth quarter compared to the same period last year.

But here's the thing: despite all these efforts, the market is still punishing Sleep Number. Their stock price has been on a rollercoaster ride, ranging from $76.83 to $80.64, with a market capitalization of $1.73 billion. The company's P/E ratio of 9.99 suggests that the stock is relatively undervalued compared to other stocks, which could be due to the recent financial performance and the challenging market environment. However, the company's adjusted EBITDA growth and improved net income could indicate that the stock is undervalued and has the potential for future growth.

So, what's the bottom line? Sleep Number is facing significant headwinds in the market, but they're not sitting idly by. They're making strategic moves to stabilize their financial position and position themselves for long-term growth. But will it be enough to turn things around? Only time will tell, folks. Stay tuned!

data.title: Td Synnex Stock Drops 2.60% Amid Fraud Probe
Highest Job Cuts Since 2020 Amid DOGE Layoffs, Putting Pressure on Friday Payroll
data.description:
The U.S. has just experienced the highest job cuts since the COVID pandemic, highlighted by aggressive layoffs from DOGE, which are putting pressure on Friday's nonfarm payroll data.

President Trump's efforts to cut down spending and Elon Musk-led DOGE initiatives are shaking the overall job market, with U.S. job cuts at their highest level in nearly five years, according to Challenger, gray & Christmas, which reported this on Thursday.

Stocks tumbled following the release, with S&P 500 futures dropping more than 1.2% and Nasdaq 100 down 1.6%.

The firm reported that U.S. employers announced 172,017 layoffs for the month, up 245% month-over-month and 103% year-over-year, marking the highest monthly count since July 2020. So far this year, employers have announced 221,812 job cuts, the highest year-to-date total since the 2009 financial crisis, when 428,099 job cuts were planned.

"Private companies announced plans to shed thousands of jobs last month, particularly in retail and technology. With the impact of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) actions, as well as canceled government contracts, fears of trade wars, and bankruptcies, job cuts soared in February," said Andrew Challenger, Senior Vice President and workplace expert for Challenger, Gray & Christmas.

More than one-third, or 62,422 announced job cuts, came from Elon Musk's efforts. The government has cut 62,530 positions, an increase of 41,311% from the 151 cuts announced through February 2024.

"It appears the administration wants to cut even more workers, but an order to fire roughly 200,000 probationary employees was blocked by a federal judge. It remains to be seen how many more workers will lose their federal government roles," said Challenger.

Retail saw 38,956 cuts for the month as companies such as macy's and Forever 21 announced sharp staff reductions, translating to a 572% increase year-over-year. Technology firms also reported another 14,554 reductions.

This data comes after significantly below-average private sector employment figures. Companies added just 77,000 new workers during the month, a sharp decline from the upwardly revised 186,000 in January and well below the 148,000 consensus estimate, according to ADP.

Investors are now closely watching Friday's nonfarm payroll report, which is expected to show job gains of 170,000 and an unemployment rate steady at 4%.

data.title: Northern Tech Stocks Soar 1.48% on Trust's Tech Boost!
Texas Senate Passes Bill to Establish Bitcoin and Digital Asset Strategic Reserve
data.description:
The Texas Senate has taken a significant step in the realm of cryptocurrency by passing Senate Bill 21 (SB-21), which establishes a Bitcoin and digital asset strategic reserve. The bill, introduced by State Senator Charles Schwertner, was passed with a 25-5 vote on March 6, 2025, after thorough deliberation on the Senate floor. Schwertner argued that the legislation would help the state shore up its balance sheet with a valuable and scarce asset, similar to gold, rather than being a direct competitor to the US dollar as a global reserve currency.

The strategic reserve legislation was initially introduced in January 2025 as a Bitcoin-only bill, but it was later refiled in February to include other digital assets. This amendment followed US President Donald Trump’s executive order directing a commission to study the feasibility of a “digital asset stockpile.” The bill aims to create a special investment fund outside of the state treasury, with Bitcoin serving as the primary source of currency. This strategic reserve would allow the Texas Comptroller to acquire, manage, and sell cryptocurrencies, providing flexibility in investment strategies and positioning Texas as a leader in cryptocurrency adoption.

The passage of SB-21 marks a significant milestone in Texas's efforts to diversify its financial assets and hedge against inflation and economic volatility. By establishing a strategic reserve for Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies, Texas aims to set a precedent for other states to follow. The bill's unanimous approval by the Senate Banking Committee earlier in February underscores the growing acceptance of cryptocurrency as a viable investment option. While other states have faced challenges with similar proposals due to concerns over cryptocurrency volatility, Texas continues to push forward with its innovative approach to financial management.

The upcoming vote on SB-21 will be crucial in determining the future of cryptocurrency in Texas and its potential impact on national financial policies. If passed, SB-21 would make Texas the first state to incorporate cryptocurrency into its financial strategy, potentially setting a precedent for other states to follow. The bill's passage would also align with broader efforts to diversify state assets and provide a hedge against inflation and economic volatility. The strategic reserve would allow the Texas Comptroller to acquire, manage, and sell cryptocurrencies, providing flexibility in investment strategies and positioning Texas as a leader in cryptocurrency adoption.

data.title: Dawgz AI Leads 2025 Crypto Wave with AI-Powered Trading
data.description:
In the ever-evolving world of cryptocurrency, new tokens are constantly emerging, offering exciting opportunities for investors. Among the latest additions to the market are Dawgz AI (DAGZ), Bonk, and Nexo, each bringing unique features and potential to the table.

Dawgz AI, launched in 2025, is an AI-powered meme coin that stands out in the crowded meme coin space. Unlike traditional meme coins, Dawgz AI combines artificial intelligence with automated trading to maximize returns. This innovative approach not only adds a layer of excitement but also provides real utility, making it a game-changer in the meme coin market. The presale for Dawgz AI raised over $2.3 million, indicating strong investor interest and confidence in its potential.

Bonk, on the other hand, is the first major dog-themed meme coin on the Solana blockchain. Launched in 2022, Bonk has gained popularity through massive airdrops that boosted its adoption and created a strong community. The Solana blockchain ensures cheap and instant transfers, making Bonk a fun and efficient token. Its community-driven growth and the early gains seen by airdrop recipients have further fueled its popularity.

Nexo, established in 2018, is a well-known player in the crypto lending industry. Unlike meme coins, Nexo offers a full lending platform that allows users to borrow against their crypto holdings and earn interest on deposits. With features such as earning up to 12% APY on crypto holdings, instant crypto loans, and regulatory compliance, Nexo provides a secure and reliable platform for crypto lending.

When comparing these three projects, Dawgz AI stands out as the most promising for 2025. Its AI-powered trading bots, staking rewards, and smart tokenomics offer both utility and excitement. While Bonk and Nexo have their unique features, Dawgz AI's innovation and potential for massive growth make it the best new crypto coin to watch.

Investors looking to capitalize on the next big wave in the crypto market should consider these fresh projects. However, it is essential to conduct independent research and understand the risks involved in trading cryptocurrencies, as the market's volatility can lead to significant losses.

data.title: "Stocks Of The Day: Are Double Bottom Patterns Bullish For Valero, Marathon?"
data.description:
Ladies and gentlemen, buckle up! Today, we're diving into the world of energy stocks and examining two giants: valero energy (VLO) and marathon petroleum (MPC). The question on everyone's mind: Are the double bottom patterns forming in these stocks a bullish signal, or just a mirage in the desert of market volatility? Let's find out!

First, let's talk about Valero Energy (VLO). This stock has been on a rollercoaster ride, with a total return of -0.34% over the past 12 months. That's a far cry from the S&P 500's 25.80% return. But here's the thing: vlo has shown some resilience, with a 2.12% YTD return. Now, if you look at the chart, you might see a double bottom pattern forming. This pattern, shaped like a "W," suggests that the stock has found support and is ready to rebound. But don't get too excited just yet!



Let's break it down. The first low was met with significant buying interest, producing a long, light candlestick and a bullish engulfing line. The subsequent high was nearly 10% up from the first low, suggesting investors should keep a sharp eye out for another downside move at this point, as rebounds from the first low are typically on the order of 10% to 20%. The second low of the pattern is within 3% to 4% of the prior low, contributing to the validity of the pattern. With the second bottom now in place, traders should reckon with a potential correction higher, or even a new uptrend, as a level of significant support has been reached and tested twice. The pattern is invalidated and downside potential resumes on a drop below the double bottom lows. On the other hand, a daily close above the intermediate high suggests a major reversal and per

But here's the kicker: VLO's financials are a mess. Revenue CAGR TTM is -10.93%, net income CAGR TTM is -65.53%, and EPS CAGR TTM is -84.68%. That's a triple whammy of bad news! So, while the double bottom pattern might be tempting, you need to be cautious. This is not a no-brainer buy!

Now, let's turn our attention to Marathon Petroleum (MPC). This stock has had a better run, with a 2.03% YTD return. But don't let that fool you. MPC's revenue for 2024 was $139.34 billion, a decrease of -6.70% compared to the previous year. Earnings were $3.44 billion, a decrease of -64.41%. Ouch! But, just like VLO, MPC might be forming a double bottom pattern. The first low was met by significant buying interest after a sudden, sharp decline, producing a long, light candlestick and a bullish engulfing line. The subsequent high is nearly 10% up from the first low, suggesting investors should keep a sharp eye out for another downside move at this point, as rebounds from the first low are typically on the order of 10% to 20%. The second low of the pattern is within 3% to 4% of the prior low, contributing to the validity of the pattern. With the second bottom now in place, traders should reckon with a potential correction higher, or even a new uptrend, as a level of significant support has been reached and tested twice. The pattern is invalidated and downside potential resumes on a drop below the double bottom lows. On the other hand, a daily close above the intermediate high suggests a major reversal and per

But here's the thing: MPC's financials are not much better than VLO's. So, while the double bottom pattern might be enticing, you need to be careful. This is not a slam-dunk buy!

In conclusion, while double bottom patterns can be a bullish signal, you need to look at the bigger picture. VLO and MPC's financials are not inspiring confidence. So, do your homework, stay vigilant, and remember: the market is a fickle beast. Don't get caught in the trap of chasing patterns without considering the fundamentals. Stay tuned for more stock picks and market insights!

data.title: Ranpak's Q4 2024 Earnings Call: Discrepancies in Automation Growth and Warranty Impacts on EBITDA
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Ranpak Holdings Corp.'s latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Automation Revenue Growth Expectations and Impact of Warranty Expenses on EBITDA:



Revenue and Volume Growth:
- Ranpak Holdings Corp. reported a 17% increase in constant currency net revenue for Q4 2024, driven by 12% volume growth.
- This significant growth was primarily due to an outstanding e-commerce-led holiday season in North America and automation's largest revenue quarter ever.

Automation and Strategic Accounts:
- Automation revenue grew by over 40% in 2024, with expectations for another 50% growth in 2025.
- The growth was attributed to the ramp-up of enterprise accounts, strategic accounts, and increased automation sales.

Capital Expenditure and Cash Flow:
- Capital expenditures declined by 40%, reaching $33.1 million in 2024.
- The reduction in CapEx is part of Ranpak's strategic shift from major investment to cash generation and debt paydown, aiming for improved financial leverage.

North America Performance:
- North America's sales increased by 36% and volumes by nearly 40% in Q4 2024.
- The strong performance was driven by increased demand in the distribution channel, with broad demand improvements in the region.
